intro.title: The Way of the Future
intro.subtitle:
Personalized software that learns from you, networks with people you trust, and actually respects your privacy.

(the way of the future... the way of the future...)

the-problem.title: The Current Situation
the-problem.body:
Your software knows everything about you. It sells that to advertisers. Your recommendations are "personalized" by training on 800M users who are kind of like you.

Meanwhile: your friend who has identical taste has recommendations you will never see.

This is fine. 🐶🔥
bullets: Spotify knows your vibe but can't tell your bestie
Netflix thinks you and your partner share a personality
Your data leaves your device and never comes back
"Personalization" = you're a cluster centroid, buddy

branch-tech-stack.title: What's your damage?
branch-tech-stack.subtitle: Let's self-select before we go deeper. Where does your brain live?

branches.label: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
branches.desc: I want to prove I know stuff without revealing stuff

branches.label: UMAP & Embeddings
branches.desc: I live in latent space and I'm not coming out

branches.label: Attribute-Based Encryption
branches.desc: Crypto but make it spicy with trust hierarchies

zkp-deep-dive.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs: Trust Me Bro, Cryptographically
zkp-deep-dive.body:
ZKPs let you prove something is true without revealing why it's true. It's the cryptographic equivalent of:

"I know where the party is. No I won't tell you. Yes you're invited. No I can't explain. Just trust."

In our system: your software can prove it shares preferences with someone without exposing your actual preference graph.
bullets: Prove taste similarity without revealing your embarrassing Nickelback phase
Verify trust claims across network without a central authority
Compose proofs: 'Alice trusts Bob who trusts Carol' — fully verifiable
zk-SNARKs: Groth16 or PLONK, ~100ms proving time on modern hardware

umap-deep-dive.title: UMAP: Your Taste Has Coordinates
umap-deep-dive.body:
Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection. Takes high-dimensional preference data and finds the actual geometric structure of your taste.

Two people close in UMAP space are genuinely similar — not just "both watched The Office."

The move: run UMAP locally, share only your manifold coordinates, not the raw data.
bullets: Preserves local topology: nearby in space = genuinely similar in taste
Run on device → coordinates leave, source data stays home
Add differential privacy noise to exported coordinates
Cross-domain: music + films + articles → one unified taste manifold
umap-deep-dive.code: import umap # Your preferences, never leaving your machine embedder = umap.UMAP(n_components=16, metric='cosine') coords = embedder.fit_transform(local_prefs) # Share coordinates, not source data network.broadcast(coords + laplace_noise(epsilon=0.1))

mabe-deep-dive.title: Multi-Authority ABE: Encryption With an Org Chart
mabe-deep-dive.body:
Normal encryption: you have a key or you don't. Boring. Binary. Boomer.

Attribute-Based Encryption: "decrypt this if you have attributes A and B, but not if you only have A."

Multi-Authority ABE: multiple independent authorities grant attributes. No single point of trust. No single point of failure. Very distributed. Much sovereignty.
bullets: "Decrypt if: (close-friend AND music-taste-similar) OR (family AND location-overlap)"
Your doctor, employer, and bestie are all separate authorities
Revoke access by attribute without re-encrypting your entire history
No master key exists. Nobody has the whole picture. Not even us.

trust-layers.title: The Trust Stack
trust-layers.body:
Three technologies. One goal: let your software network with the people you actually trust, at the level you actually trust them.

They were made for each other. Nobody had put them together yet. We're putting them together.
bullets: 🔐 ZKPs — prove similarity claims without revealing underlying data
🗺️  UMAP — locate yourself in shared taste-space, privately
🏛️  Multi-authority ABE — encrypt content for exactly the right trust tier
Composed: your software collaborates with your network's software

trust-network.title: Networked Personalization, Actually
trust-network.body:
Imagine: your music app shares a preference signal with your friend's music app — not through Spotify's servers, not through anyone's servers — peer to peer, cryptographically scoped to your trust relationship.

Their app improves because yours knows something. Yours improves because theirs does. The network gets smarter as a unit.

This is federated learning but make it personal and actually give you the control.
bullets: Peer-to-peer model updates scoped by trust level
Close friends: high-fidelity signal sharing
Acquaintances: aggregated, noisy, still useful
Strangers: fully homomorphically encrypted vibes only

branch-demo-mode.title: How do you want to see this?
branch-demo-mode.subtitle: We can go technical or we can go feelings. No judgment. (Some judgment.)

branches.label: Show me the demo
branches.desc: I need to see it running or I don't believe you

branches.label: Walk me through the architecture
branches.desc: Explain the system design like I'm a suspicious engineer

demo-live.title: Demo: Two Instances, One Vibe
demo-live.body:
Two isolated software instances. They share nothing except a trust relationship and a ZK-proven taste similarity.

Instance A rates three albums. Instance B has rated zero albums. Instance B now has recommendations. No server involved. No raw data crossed the wire — only a proof and a coordinate vector.

They're the same picture. No wait, they're not. That's the point.
bullets: Step 1: A generates UMAP embedding from local interaction history
Step 2: A creates ZK proof that embedding is valid (not adversarial noise)
Step 3: B verifies proof, updates its local model with A's gradient signal
Step 4: B recommends something A would have predicted — without knowing A's data

demo-theory.title: Architecture: The Paranoid's Dream Stack
demo-theory.body:
Each node is a personal software agent — runs locally, owns its data, exposes only proofs and encrypted signals to the network.

The network is a signed directed trust graph. Edges carry trust-level metadata encrypted under MA-ABE. Nobody can read your edge weights except the named participants.
bullets: Local store: raw preferences — never leaves device
Proof layer: zk-SNARK circuit over UMAP embedding validity
Network layer: libp2p gossip, ABE-encrypted payloads
Learning layer: federated gradient updates, scoped by trust edge weight
demo-theory.code: // Every message in the network looks like this { "from": "[ZK identity — not your name]", "proof": "[I am who I claim, I share what I claim]", "payload": "[ABE-encrypted gradient update]", "recipients": "[a policy, not a list]" }

auto-improve.title: Auto-Improving: The Software Gym
auto-improve.body:
The system doesn't just share recommendations. It updates its own model from your network's collective signal.

Technically: federated gradient descent, but the federation is your personal trust graph — not a corporation's server farm running on your behavioral exhaust.

"They told me I could become anything. So I became a locally-improving preference model with cryptographic guarantees."
bullets: Each user interaction → local gradient update
Trust-weighted aggregation from network neighbors
Concept drift detection: your taste changes, the model follows
No catastrophic forgetting — you remain yourself, just better-served

branch-future-vision.title: Be honest. How do you feel about this?
branch-future-vision.subtitle: This is a judgment-free zone. (It is not a judgment-free zone.)

branches.label: I'm excited, let's build
branches.desc: Take my gradients. This is the way.

branches.label: I have serious concerns
branches.desc: Who watches the watchmen? What about adversarial proofs?

optimist-future.title: The World Where This Wins
optimist-future.body:
Software that improves because of your relationships, not despite them. Music apps. Reading apps. News feeds. Health trackers. All learning from trust networks rather than anonymous behavioral slurry.

The surveillance economy breaks down when your data never leaves your device but your preferences still propagate through your social graph — cryptographically, consensually, reversibly.
bullets: Your taste shapes your network's software — mutual benefit, zero extraction
Small trust networks can out-personalize massive platforms on niche domains
Communities of interest become self-improving, self-sovereign knowledge graphs
The protocol is open — run it on anything, trust whoever you choose

skeptic-future.title: Your Concerns Are Valid and We Anticipated Them
skeptic-future.body:
"Adversarial proofs?" — The ZK circuit validates the embedding was derived from real interaction data. Poisoning requires compromising your own device first.

"What if I trust someone wrong?" — Trust is revocable. ABE attribute revocation propagates without re-encrypting history. You can un-trust someone and your model forgets their signal.

"Sybil attacks?" — Great question. Web-of-trust bootstrapping plus optional proof-of-personhood integration. Working on it. No deck hides everything.
bullets: Adversarial resistance: circuit enforces data provenance at proof time
Trust revocation: first-class primitive, not a bolt-on afterthought
Sybil resistance: WoT bootstrapping + optional PoP for high-stakes edges
Open source: if you don't trust us, read the circuit constraints yourself

convergence-close.title: Where We Actually Are
convergence-close.body:
Research prototype. The primitives are real and proven individually. The gap is composition — making them work together at consumer speed and scale.

That's the interesting problem. That's what we're working on. Surprised Pikachu face if it were easy.
bullets: UMAP ✅ — ships in production today
zk-SNARKs ✅ — Groth16 proving ~100ms on modern hardware (see Zcash, Aztec)
MA-ABE ⚠️ — fast enough for hourly syncs, not yet real-time
Full composition 🔨 — that's the research gap we're closing

vision-statement.title: The Bet
vision-statement.body:
Current model: your data feeds someone else's model. You get marginally better recommendations. They get a moat and a valuation.

Proposed model: your data feeds your model, which shares signals with people you trust. Everyone in your network gets better recommendations. Nobody builds a moat from your behavior.

Same math. Different who benefits. Different power structure falls out of the data flow.
bullets: Centralized personalization → platform capture, behavioral lock-in
Trust-network personalization → distributed sovereignty, mutual benefit
The technology is not the hard part — the defaults are
The way of the future... the way of the future...

outro.title: The Way of the Future
outro.subtitle:
Build software that belongs to you.
Share it with people you trust.
Prove everything. Reveal nothing.
